bids.reasoning: I am targeting a very high-utility bid at the opening stage, since time pressure is nonexistent at t=0.00 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me only moderate utility, and their message is aggressive cheap talk, so I will make only a small concession on the lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the three high-importance issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent’s second offer actually reduced my utility from about 0.442 to 0.397, so I should not make large concessions based on their aggressive messages. I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.877 while avoiding an exact repeat of my prior offer, conceding on issueC from my best value to my second-best while holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while keeping a small concession on the low-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers have not improved for my utility and their messages are aggressive cheap talk rather than evidence of real concession, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues while avoiding an exact repeat of my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers have not improved in utility to me, hovering around the same low range despite aggressive messages. I am therefore targeting a high-utility bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer, keeping my strongest issues mostly firm and maintaining the low-weight issueD concession the opponent has consistently requested.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a visible concession on the lowest-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers remain low utility for me and do not show a consistent concession trend, while their messages are mostly pressure tactics, so I will not concede on the high-weight issues yet.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid rather than making major concessions; the opponent’s offers remain low for me and do not show a clear improving trend. Their messages complain about issueA and issueB, but the actual bids suggest they are holding firm, so I will make only a modest concession by moving issueC off my top value while keeping my highest-weight priorities strong.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility offer while making a visible concession to avoid simply repeating my prior bids. The opponent’s valid offers have not shown consistent utility improvement for me and their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but they repeatedly signal concern over issueA/issueB, so I concede on issueA while holding issueB and issueC, which are highly valuable to me.

I am targeting a high but modestly more conciliatory utility around 0.79, since we are still early at t=0.14 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s bids remain low-value for me, though their latest offer moved issueA to valueB; their messages demand concessions on issueA/issueB, so I am conceding on issueB while holding my strongest priorities on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong early-game utility around 0.75, since time is only 0.16 and the opponent’s actual offers have not improved for me despite their pressure messages. Their bids repeatedly avoid my top values on issueB and issueC, so I am making a limited concession on issueB and the low-weight issueD while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid (about 0.77) because it is still early at t=0.18, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s offers are not clearly improving for me, but they have repeatedly pushed against my positions on issueA and issueB and recently offered issueA=valueB, so I concede issueA while holding firm on high-value issueB and issueC. I also vary issueD to avoid repeating my previous bids and use it as a lower-weight concession.

I am targeting a still-solid utility of about 0.64 while making a visible concession on issueA and issueB, which the opponent repeatedly emphasizes in both bids and messages. Since time is only at 0.20, I do not need to drop to their latest low-utility offer, but their recent movement toward issueA:valueB suggests this may be a more credible compromise point.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, which is a meaningful concession from my ideal while still protecting the high-weight issues at this early/mid stage. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their bids show some movement, especially their latest willingness to put issueB at valueA; I will reciprocate by conceding issueA to valueB and issueD to valueC while moving issueC to a moderate valueA. This avoids simply repeating my last bid and keeps the proposal within the valid domain.

I am targeting a solid mid-high utility around 0.64 by conceding on issueA and issueB while holding issueC at my top value, since it is one of my highest-weight issues. At t=0.24 there is some room to move, but it is still early enough not to match the opponent’s much lower-utility offers; their messages are aggressive, but their bids remain mostly low for me and only oscillate rather than steadily improve.

At t=0.26 there is still time, so I am targeting a solid positive utility while making visible concessions on issues the opponent keeps emphasizing. Their actual offers suggest they may tolerate issueB: valueA, while they often push away from my preferred issueC: valueB, so I concede issueC to valueC and keep a compromise on issueA rather than fully yielding.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.62: it preserves issueB=valueA, which is highly valuable to me and which the opponent has recently signaled willingness to offer, while conceding on issueC and low-weight issueD. At t=0.28 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers have recently become more valuable to me, so I am making a real concession without dropping too far. Their aggressive messages are treated as cheap talk, but their recent bids suggest issueB=valueA and issueC=valueC may be workable.

I am targeting a solid but more conciliatory utility around 0.66 at t=0.30, since we are entering the mid-game but not yet under deadline pressure. The opponent’s actual bids show some movement on issueB toward my preferred valueA, while they continue pushing lower outcomes for issueA and issueC; their messages are aggressive but mostly cheap-talk pressure, so I am conceding on issueA and issueC while holding issueB where it is valuable to me.
